item.title: Why OpenAI really shut down Sora
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: OpenAI's decision last week to shut down Sora, its AI video-generation tool, just six months after releasing it to the public raised immediate suspicions. The app had invited users to upload their own faces — so was this some kind of elaborate data grab?

item.title: The Pixel 10a doesn’t have a camera bump, and it’s great
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: The Google Pixel 10a can lay flat on a table, but otherwise the company hasn't brought a ton of upgrades to its newest budget smartphone.

item.title: YouTube CEO says the best YouTubers will ‘never leave their home’
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: CEO Neal Mohan insisted that he isn’t worried about Netflix luring away YouTube's most popular creators.

item.title: ‘Project Hail Mary’ becomes Amazon MGM’s biggest box office hit
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Amazon’s bet on “Project Hail Mary” has paid off handsomely.

item.title: Sora’s shutdown could be a reality check moment for AI video
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Is this just normal corporate strategy, or are we about to see a broader pullback on AI-generated video?

item.title: TechCrunch Mobility: When a robotaxi has to call 911
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility — your central hub for news and insights on the future of transportation.

item.title: SXSW rebounds as a top networking, ideas festival for founders and VCs
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: SXSW made some changes this year, but some founders loved it all the same.

item.title: Bluesky leans into AI with Attie, an app for building custom feeds
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Bluesky’s new app Attie uses AI to help people build custom feeds the open social networking protocol atproto.

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg texted Elon Musk to offer help with DOGE
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: While the relationship was once thorny enough that Musk challenged Zuckerberg to a cagefight, things seem to have warmed up by the early days of the second Trump administration.

item.title: Stanford study outlines dangers of asking AI chatbots for personal advice
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: While there’s been plenty of debate about AI sycophancy, a new study by Stanford computer scientists attempts to measure how harmful that tendency might be.

item.title: Elon Musk’s last co-founder reportedly leaves xAI
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: All but two of Musk's 11 xAI co-founders departed before this week.

item.title: These iPad apps will make you wish you had more free time
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: We’ve compiled a list of some of the best iPad apps for creativity that are available on the App Store.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude popularity with paying consumers is skyrocketing
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Estimates for total Claude consumer users are all over the map (we've seen figures ranging from 18 million to 30 million). Anthropic hasn't disclosed this data, but a spokesperson did tell TechCrunch that Claude paid subscriptions have more than doubled this year.

item.title: What will power the grid in 2035? The race is wide open
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Fusion, fission, and even natural gas are appeared tied in the race to deliver new power to the grid in the early 2030s.

item.title: Let’s take a look at the retro tech making a comeback
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Boomboxes, instant cameras, and even landlines are making a comeback. Here are the coolest retro-inspired devices available.

item.title: From Moon hotels to cattle herding: 8 startups investors chased at YC Demo Day
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: We polled nearly a dozen VCs to find out which W26 startups are the sought after in the batch.

item.title: Whoop’s fitness band is cool. Can it stay cool as the company grows?
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Whoop founder Will Ahmed has spent 14 years building a health wearable beloved by elite athletes, and is now racing Oura — and the FDA, and the limits of consumer medicine — to turn it into something that could one day save your life.

item.title: Physical Intelligence is reportedly in talks to raise $1 billion, again
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: The deal would effectively double the company's $5.6 billion valuation in just four months.

item.title: Why SoftBank’s new $40B loan points to a 2026 OpenAI IPO
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Wall Street giants JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs are extending a 12-month, unsecured loan to the Japanese conglomerate.

item.title: Memory chip giant SK hynix could help end ‘RAMmageddon’ with blockbuster US IPO
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: SK hynix’s potential U.S. listing could raise $10-$14 billion to help it build more capacity, encourage others to follow, and end the 'RAMmageddon' memory shortage.
